incorrects.description: Student selected 1 — may have miscounted or confused scale value with difference

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Count all the symbols in each row. Squirrels has 10, Birds has 7. How many fewer is 7 than 10?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 — may have miscounted one or both bars

steps.text: Here's a hint: Count the Squirrels row carefully. Each symbol is worth 2 sightings. How many symbols do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 7 — reported the Birds value instead of finding the difference

steps.text: You're working on it. 7 is how many Birds were sighted. But the question asks how many FEWER—that means we subtract: 10 minus 7.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System highlights Squirrels row (5 symbols × 2 = 10), then Birds row (3.5 symbols × 2 = 7), then shows subtraction 10 - 7 = 3

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Squirrels has 5 symbols—each worth 2, so that's 10 total. Birds has 3 and a half symbols—that's 7 total. To find how many fewer, we subtract: 10 minus 7 equals 3.
